blurb:
This little Middle Eastern spot in Franklin Gateway has become our go-to for quick, fresh food that doesn't break the bank. It's casual counter service in a strip mall, but the kitchen clearly knows what they're doing. Great for lunch or an easy weeknight dinner when you want something different from the usual chains.

Their shawarma is what keeps us coming back, and the falafel is crispy outside and fluffy inside. The portions are generous and everything comes with their tahini sauce that my kids actually eat. They also have good veggie options if you're trying to eat lighter.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This food hall right on the Square is perfect when your family can't agree on what to eat. Twenty different vendors under one roof means everyone gets what they want, and there's plenty of seating in the middle. Great for lunch downtown or when you're hitting the farmers market and want to grab a bite.

The variety changes but you'll usually find everything from tacos to Asian food to pizza. It's also nice that some of the vendors sell local products and gifts, so you can browse while you eat. The atmosphere is lively but not too loud, and parking is easier than some other spots on the Square.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is a fun place to try it without the downtown Atlanta prices. It's in the Powers Ferry shopping center and has that relaxed family restaurant vibe. Perfect for celebrations or when you want to try something different with a group.

The cheese bread alone is worth the trip, and they keep bringing different cuts of barbecued meat to your table until you flip your card to red. The feijoada stew is hearty and authentic if you want to branch out from just the grilled meats. They also do breakfast, which is unusual and pretty good if you're in the area early.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is my go-to spot when I want really good Indian food without the drive into Decatur or downtown. It's in a strip mall in Perimeter Center, but don't let that fool you. The colorful dining room has a warm family vibe, and the portions are generous enough to share. Perfect for introducing kids to Indian food or when you want comfort food that's different from the usual.

The biryani is what they're known for, and it lives up to the name on the door. The chicken biryani comes in a huge portion with perfectly spiced rice and tender meat. I also love their family-style approach where you can order a few dishes and everyone tries everything. The dal and naan are solid choices to round out a meal.

Just note they close between lunch and dinner most weekdays, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Weekend hours are longer, which is nice for a leisurely Saturday dinner.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to all the hype you've probably heard. The atmosphere is vibrant and fun, perfect for girls' night out or when you want good Mexican food with friends. The service is really attentive, which is nice when you're trying to decide between all their creative drink options. It's in Sandy Springs Place, so easy parking and you can walk around after dinner.

Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and gets the table sharing. The drink pitchers are where they really shine - try the Pineapple Guava or Passion Fruit pitcher if you're with a group. They balance the alcohol and sweetness perfectly, not too sugary like some places. The tacos themselves are solid, and portions are good for the price.

They're open late most nights which is great when you want decent food after 10pm. Just know it gets loud and busy on weekends, so go early if you want to actually have a conversation.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
I was skeptical about another pizza place until I tried this one. The crust is genuinely different - thin and crispy but still has some chew to it, not like cardboard. It's in the City Springs development, so you can grab dinner and walk around or catch a movie after. Good for date night or when the kids want pizza but you want something that doesn't taste like chain restaurant food.

The ingredients really are better than most pizza places around here. They use local stuff when possible and import some things from Italy, which sounds fancy but you can actually taste the difference. The Margherita is perfect if you want to see what they're about, and the prosciutto pizza is my personal favorite.

It gets busy on weekends, so either go early or be prepared to wait a bit. The salads are surprisingly good too if someone in your group isn't in a pizza mood.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is officially on my top 5 dinner places in Atlanta, and I don't say that lightly. A friend dragged me here for girls' night and I cannot wait to go back. The Persian food is incredible, and the cocktails are creative without being weird. It's perfect for date night or when you want to try something different that's still really approachable. The service makes you feel like a regular even on your first visit.

Get the hummus to start, then the chicken shish kabobs with the rice that has lentils and raisins. That rice sounds weird but it's amazing - slightly sweet and savory at the same time. The wings are also surprisingly good if someone in your group wants something more familiar. Save room for the tiramisu because it's the perfect end to the meal.

They break between lunch and dinner most days, so check the hours before you go. Weekend dinner reservations are probably a good idea because word is definitely getting out about this place.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This French bakery is the real deal and always packed for good reason. It's bright and cheerful inside with cases full of pastries and the smell of fresh bread. Perfect for weekend brunch or when you need a good birthday cake that actually tastes as good as it looks.

Their croissants are buttery and flaky like you'd get in Paris, and the quiche is excellent for a light lunch. The petits fours are beautiful for special occasions, and they make proper French baguettes if you're cooking at home. Just know they close early on Sundays and the popular items sell out, so don't wait until the afternoon if you want the best selection.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This is the diner that never sleeps, literally. Open 24/7 on Cobb Parkway, it's where we go for late night cravings or when the kids want breakfast at 3pm on a Saturday. The neon sign out front makes it easy to spot, and inside it's classic diner booth seating with that familiar hum of conversation.

The menu is massive and includes Greek dishes alongside typical diner fare. Their gyro platter is surprisingly good, and the breakfast portions are huge. We usually stick to the basics like pancakes or a burger, but I've seen people order everything from spanakopita to chicken and waffles. Perfect for when you need food at odd hours or want that comfort food experience without worrying about what time it is.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is where we take out-of-town guests when we want to show off Atlanta. The setting right on the Chattahoochee River is gorgeous, especially if you can snag a table on the patio at sunset. It's definitely a special occasion place with the prices to match, but the food and service make it worth it for anniversaries or milestone celebrations.

The Southern seafood is what Ray's does best. Their shrimp and grits is probably the most popular dish, and the grouper is always fresh and perfectly prepared. The wine list is extensive if you're into that, and the cocktails are well-made. It feels fancy without being stuffy.

Make a reservation, especially for weekend dinner or Sunday brunch. The Sunday brunch is actually a great way to experience the place without the full dinner price tag.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
